dialogue.utterance: Unit 3, respond to a call for a fall with possible head injury and brief loss of consciousness. Location is [CITY] Town Center.
Hello, I'm Brian. I'm an EMT. Can you tell me what happened and how you're feeling right now?
He was rock-hopping with his sons and slipped, hit his head, and lost consciousness briefly. Now he has a dull, localized headache at the back of his head, about a 5/10 that worsens with movement.
Got it. He's alert and oriented, no reported chest pain or shortness of breath, and has been placed in a cervical collar and Stokes basket by first responders.
Yes, and he looked a bit confused when he first came around, but was speaking clearly and walked strangely before being immobilized.
Justin, can you tell me your name and where we are?
My name is Justin. I think we're in [CITY].
You came to [CITY] with your sons. Can you tell me when you slipped?
It was just a minute ago while I was playing on the rocks with them.
On a scale of 1 to 4, how alert and oriented do you feel right now?
Hmm... I feel a little foggy but I'm awake and understand what's happening.
Justin, are you having trouble breathing, chest pain, dizziness, or any vision changes right now?
No chest pain or shortness of breath. No dizziness or vision problems. I just have a headache.
What were you doing right before you fell?
He was jumping across rocks with his sons when he slipped. He hit his head when he fell.
Do you have any ongoing medical conditions or medications?
I have high blood pressure, type 2 diabetes, and GERD. I take rosuvastatin and omeprazole.
On a scale from 0 to 10, how bad is your head pain right now?
It's a 5. Dull and constant.
When did you first notice the pain?
Right after I hit the ground. It gets worse when we move or hit bumps.
Was the onset sudden or gradual?
Sudden. I felt it right when I hit my head, and it's been there since.
Justin, can you talk? Are there any issues with your breathing or throat?
I can talk and breathe fine.
Take a deep breath for me. Any trouble breathing or shortness of breath?
No.
Your breathing is fine. Rhythm and depth are normal, bilateral breath sounds are clear.
Pulse is strong and regular in both arms. Skin is warm, dry, no cyanosis noted. Any chest pain or discomfort?
No chest pain or discomfort.
Any swelling, redness, or tenderness in your arms, legs, or hands?
No, I don't feel any pain or swelling anywhere else.
Can I examine your pupils? I'll use a light if that's okay.
Fine.
Pupils are equal, round, and reactive to light.
Do you feel any pain, stiffness, or pressure in your neck?
No pain or stiffness.
No tenderness or deformity in your cervical spine. Motion looks unrestricted and stable.
I don't see any chest deforming. Breath sounds are symmetrical and clear. Diaphragm movement is normal.
Let me test your motor function in your arms and legs. Can I do that?
Yes.
Strength in all extremities is equal and strong. No deformity or abnormalities noted.
Are you experiencing any numbness or tingling?
No numbness or tingling. All limbs feel functional.
No JVD observed. Breathing is clear bilaterally with no signs of distress.
Your pulse is 108, respirations are at 16 per minute, and your blood pressure is 126/78.
SpO2 is 97% and blood sugar is 114 mg/dL.
EKG is normal sinus rhythm right now.
Justin, we've placed a working IV in your left arm. We'll continue to monitor it during transport.
IV is secure and EKG is stable. They're all functioning correctly.
Let's test your recognition. What is the current date and year?
I think it's 2009. I was playing with my sons earlier.
You're oriented to self and time but not location or event. GCS is 15 (Eyes 4, Verbal 5, Motor 6).
Justin, no cardiac pain noted, and breathing remains clear with bilateral breath sounds.
You display no signs of shortness of breath or difficulty. Chest movement is normal.
No signs of underlying fractures, and no obvious DCAP-BTLS on any extremities.
First responders mentioned some head bleeding that is currently controlled. I'll inspect that now.
There's a small abrasion bandaged at the back of your head, no blood or torn skin otherwise noted.
I started a 4-lead EKG on standby as a monitoring precaution due to your trauma.
Blood sugar was taken as a baseline but subsequent readings showed zero-no further testing needed right now.
Per protocol, we'll follow injury - head (protocol 4 - 6). Transport Justin to [COMPANY_NAME], who are ready for a trauma patient.
I'll notify the hospital with a pre-arrival report including injuries and vitals.
Let's carefully transfer Justin to the stretcher and secure him for transport.
Second reading is BP 116/77, pulse 99, and respirations 16.
SpO2 is at 95% with normal sinus rhythm on EKG.
Has your head pain improved, worsened, or stayed the same?
Still about a 5. It's been consistent.
Any new symptoms like nausea, dizziness, or confusion since the last check?
No, still just the headache and confusion about what happened.
Final vitals: heart rate 104, blood pressure 136/79, and SpO2 93%. Still normal sinus rhythm.
IV remains patent, EKG is steady. No issues noted to flag.
One more awareness check. What is the current year?
I don't know the year yet, but my name is definitely Justin.
We're all set for transport. Justin, we're moving you now toward [COMPANY_NAME] to get thoroughly assessed.
Secure. We are heading to the hospital now.
Blood pressure is 136/79. No other vital changes recorded during transport.
SpO2 is 93 and rhythm remains normal.
IV secure, good integrity in place.
No photophobia or vomiting. Your head injury response is consistent. Alert, confused, oriented to self only.
Breathing remains clear with no signs of distress. Abdomen is non-distended and no rigidity.
Justin, can you move all four limbs when I ask?
I can, as asked.
Negative for cervical injury. PMs remain positive and equal across all limbs.
GCS re-check: Eyes 4, Verbal 2, Motor 4. Total GCS score of 10 noted.
Justin, do you know who you are? And do you know where you are now?
I know my name is Justin, but I'm not sure where I am.
Normal for head trauma. Justin is aox2 and presents with partial confusion.
Where exactly is the source of the pain on your head?
Focuses on the back, around the crown of my head.
Anything that seems to trigger it or reduce the intensity at all?
No consistent changes. It just stays.
There's an abrasion on the back of your head already taped. Any other rashes, bruises, or lesions?
No. My skin feels clean elsewhere.
Any swallowing trouble or dizziness noticed during evaluation?
Nothing like that. Stable and no discomfort there.
One memory test: Can you recall what you were doing right before losing awareness?
Playing with my sons on the rocks, and after that, everything became jumbled in my mind.
You remain stable. Everything checks out fine with no new issues.
IV functioning fine. No bleeding or issues with movement as observed.
All checks end positive-IV secure, bleeding controlled, patient stable.
Describe your understanding of how you ended up here.
I was with my sons out on rocks. Next, I became confused and landed here.
Justin is being transported under Head Injury Protocol 4-6 as set. Destination is [COMPANY_NAME] hospital.
IV, monitoring, and patient are all locked in place. Adjusting environment if needed.
Reached out to the hospital with our final vitals and status.
Final on-scene vitals: BP 136/79, HR 104, RR 16, O2 93, rhythm normal.
Transporting securely, no injury exacerbation during movement expected.
We are easing into transport. Justin, if anything changes, let us know.
If I must go anywhere, this is a welcomed transfer than staying rock-limbed.
